topic.name: Cricket Legends and Their Impact
topic.description: This topic encompasses the careers and contributions of notable cricketers who have left a significant mark on the sport, particularly focusing on their achievements and legacies.

Document 1: Bob Willis, known as an aggressive fast bowler, took 325 Test wickets for England.
Willis was Wisden Cricketer of the Year for 1978.
Document 2: Lindsay Hassett was an elegant middle-order batsman for Australia, described as a master of nearly every stroke.
Hassett played a crucial role in Australia’s win in the Fourth Test during the 1938 tour of England.
Document 3: Irfan Pathan was instrumental in India's series wins in Pakistan in 2004 and was named the ICC Emerging Player of the Year in 2004.
Pathan evoked comparisons with Wasim Akram for his promising performances.
Document 4: Ray Lindwall is regarded as one of the greatest fast bowlers of all time and played a significant role in Australia’s cricket history.
Lindwall formed a legendary new-ball pairing with Keith Miller.
Document 5: Keith Miller is widely regarded as Australia’s greatest all-rounder, known for his powerful batting and versatile bowling.
Miller was a member of the record-breaking Invincibles.
Document 6: Ian Johnson was part of the famous Australian cricket team that toured England in 1948, known as The Invincibles.
Johnson had more success in tour matches, taking 85 wickets in all first-class fixtures.
Document 7: The Australian cricket team in 1948, known as The Invincibles, is regarded as one of the greatest cricket teams of all time.
The team won the Test series 4 – 0 with one draw, showcasing a formidable batting and bowling lineup.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling Careers
topic.description: This topic encompasses the professional wrestling careers of various notable wrestlers, highlighting their achievements, championships, and contributions to the wrestling industry.

Document 1: Jeff Hardy is a professional wrestler, singer-songwriter, painter and musician currently signed to Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA).

He is a 12-time world champion, having held the WWE Championship eight times.
Document 6: Matt Hardy is an American professional wrestler and wrestling promoter currently signed with TNA.
He is a nine-time world tag team champion and a three-time world champion.
Document 7: Edge is a Canadian actor and retired professional wrestler best known for his time with WWE.
He won 31 championships in WWE overall, including 11 world championships.

topic.name: State Highways in the U.S.
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways across different states in the U.S., detailing their routes, history, and maintenance.

The highway is maintained by the Montana Department of Transportation (MDT).
Document 2: M-239 is a north-south state trunkline highway in Berrien County in the extreme southwestern corner of the US state of Michigan.
M-239 is maintained by the Michigan Department of Transportation (MDOT).

The road was built in the late 1930s and established as a state highway in the 1990s.

Conn. M-13 is maintained by MDOT like other state highways in Michigan.
Document 7: K-34 is a 29.26-mile-long state highway in southwestern Kansas.
It is a two-lane road for its entire length.

topic.name: British Battlecruisers of WWI
topic.description: The documents discuss the design, development, and operational history of British battlecruisers during World War I, highlighting their roles and the evolution of naval warfare.

1: The Lion class were a class of battlecruisers built for the British Royal Navy before World War I.
Lion served as the flagship of the Grand Fleet's battlecruisers throughout World War I.
2: The three Invincible-class battlecruisers were built for the Royal Navy and entered service in 1908 as the world's first battlecruisers.
They were least successful when standing in the main line of battle where they faced enemy battleships.
3: The Admiral-class battlecruisers were to have been a class of four British Royal Navy battlecruisers designed near the end of World War I.
No more battlecruisers would be built due to the arms limitations agreements of the interbellum.
5: A battlecruiser was a capital ship built in the first half of the 20th century.
Battlecruisers served in the navies of Britain, Germany, the Ottoman Empire, Australia and Japan during World War I.

topic.name: Governors of Kentucky and West Virginia
topic.description: The documents discuss the lives and political careers of various governors and political figures from Kentucky and West Virginia during the 19th century.

Document 1: Beriah Magoffin was the 21st Governor of Kentucky, serving during the early part of the Civil War.
Magoffin adhered to a states' rights position, including the right of a state to secede from the Union.
Document 3: Lazarus Whitehead Powell was the 19th Governor of Kentucky, serving from 1851 to 1855.
Powell's reforms during his term gave Kentucky one of the top educational systems in the antebellum South.
Document 4: Preston Leslie was the 26th Governor of Kentucky from 1871 to 1875.
Leslie was a Confederate sympathizer during the Civil War, but began to adopt a more progressive position.
Document 2: Alexander W. Monroe was a prominent American lawyer, politician, and military officer in Virginia and West Virginia.
Monroe served as a member of the Virginia House of Delegates and the West Virginia House of Delegates.

topic.name: Marine Life and Adaptations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various species of marine animals, their characteristics, behaviors, and adaptations in different aquatic environments.

Document 1: Copper shark is a species of requiem shark found mostly at temperate latitudes.
Feeding mainly on cephalopods, bony fishes, and other cartilaginous fishes.
Document 2: Portia fimbriata is a jumping spider found in Australia and Southeast Asia.
The hunting tactics of Portia are versatile and adaptable.
Document 3: The external morphology of Lepidoptera is the physiological structure of butterflies and moths.
Lepidopterans undergo complete metamorphosis, going through a four-stage life cycle.
Document 4: Mimicry is a similarity of one species to another that protects one or both.
Mimics occur in the same areas as their models.
Document 5: Bigfin reef squid is a commercially important species of loliginid squid.
They exhibit elaborate mating displays and usually spawn in May.
Document 6: The dusky shark is a species of requiem shark occurring in tropical and warm-temperate continental seas worldwide.
A generalist apex predator, the dusky shark can be found from the coast to the outer continental shelf.
Document 7: Sharks are a group of fish characterized by a cartilaginous skeleton and five to seven gill slits.
Modern sharks are classified within the clade Selachimorpha.

topic.name: Cities in the United States

Document 2: Omak is a city located in the foothills of the Okanogan Highlands in north-central Washington, United States.

Document 3: Teaneck is a township in Bergen County, New Jersey, United States, and a suburb in the New York metropolitan area.
As of the 2010 United States Census, the township's population was 39,776.
Document 4: Albany is the capital of the U.S. state of New York and the seat of Albany County.
The population of the City of Albany was 97,856 according to the 2010 census.
Document 5: Manhattan is the most densely populated borough of New York City, its economic and administrative center.
Manhattan is often described as the cultural and financial capital of the world.
Document 7: Newark is the largest city (by population) in the U.S. state of New Jersey, and the county seat of Essex County.

topic.name: Evolution of Rock Music
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development and transformation of various rock music genres from the 1960s to the 2000s, highlighting key artists and influences.

Document 1: All Things Must Pass is a triple album by English musician George Harrison.
The album reflects the influence of his musical activities with artists such as Bob Dylan, the Band, Delaney & Bonnie and Billy Preston during 1968 – 70.
Document 2: Hard rock or heavy rock is a loosely defined subgenre of rock music that began in the mid-1960s.
It developed into a major form of popular music in the 1970s, with bands such as Led Zeppelin, The Who, Deep Purple, Aerosmith, AC/DC and Van Halen.
Document 3: Psychedelic rock is a style of rock music that is inspired or influenced by psychedelic culture.
It was pioneered by musicians including the Beatles, the Beach Boys, and the Byrds, emerging as a genre during the mid-1960s.
Document 4: Graduation marked a movement away from the ornate, soul-based sound of West's previous releases toward a more anthemic style.
Inspired by indie rock and Chicago house, it incorporated synthesizer sounds into his production.
Document 5: British rhythm and blues was a musical movement that developed in the United Kingdom between the late 1950s and the early 1960s.
Major acts included the Rolling Stones, Manfred Mann, The Animals, The Yardbirds.
Document 6: Progressive rock originated in the United Kingdom and United States throughout the mid-to-late 1960s.
It developed from psychedelic rock, and began as an attempt to give greater artistic weight and credibility to rock music.
Document 7: Garage rock is a raw and energetic style of rock and roll that flourished in the mid-1960s.
The style, a precursor to psychedelic rock, is often characterized by aggressive and unsophisticated lyrics and delivery.

topic.name: Equine and Plant Biology
topic.description: This topic encompasses the study of horse breeds and plant species, focusing on their characteristics, origins, and significance in their respective ecosystems.

1: The Finnhorse is a horse breed with both riding horse and draught horse influences and characteristics.

2: Narcissus is a genus of predominantly spring perennial plants in the Amaryllidaceae family.
The genus arose some time in the Late Oligocene to Early Miocene epochs.
3: Botany is the science of plant life and a branch of biology.

4: Israeli cuisine comprises local dishes by people native to Israel and dishes brought to Israel by Jews from the Diaspora.
Israeli cuisine has adopted elements of various styles of Jewish cuisine.
5: A tree is a perennial plant with an elongated stem, or trunk, supporting branches and leaves.
Trees tend to be long-lived, some reaching several thousand years old.
6: A cactus is a member of the plant family Cactaceae, comprising ca 127 genera with some 1750 known species.
Cacti show many adaptations to conserve water.
7: The Arabian horse is a breed that originated on the Arabian Peninsula.
Arabian bloodlines are found in almost every modern breed of riding horse.

topic.name: Political Leaders and Movements
topic.description: The documents discuss influential political leaders and movements in various countries, highlighting their roles, ideologies, and impacts on national and international politics.

Document 1: Hezbollah is a Shi'a Islamist militant group and political party based in Lebanon.
Hezbollah was conceived by Muslim clerics and funded by Iran primarily to harass the Israeli occupation.
Document 2: Asif Ali Zardari is a Pakistani politician and current co-chairperson of Pakistan People 's Party.
He served as the 11th President of Pakistan from 2008 to 2013.
Document 3: Gamal Abdel Nasser was the second President of Egypt, serving from 1956 until his death.
Nasser led the 1952 overthrow of the monarchy and introduced far-reaching land reforms.
Document 4: Ken Livingstone is an English politician who served as the Leader of the Greater London Council.
He was a vocal opponent of the Conservative Party government.
Document 5: Stephen Harper is a Canadian politician and member of Parliament who served as the 22nd Prime Minister of Canada.
He was the first prime minister to come from the modern Conservative Party of Canada.
Document 6: Joseph McCarthy was an American politician who served as a U.S. Senator from Wisconsin.
He became the most visible public face of a period in which Cold War tensions fueled fears of widespread Communist subversion.
Document 7: Fidel Castro governed the Republic of Cuba as Prime Minister from 1959 to 1976 and then as President from 1976 to 2008.
Under his administration Cuba became a one-party communist state.

topic.name: Military Aviation Technology
topic.description: The documents discuss various military aircraft, their development, capabilities, and roles within the armed forces.

1: The General Dynamics F-16 Fighting Falcon is a single-engine multirole fighter aircraft originally developed by General Dynamics for the United States Air Force.
The F-16 has key features including a frameless bubble canopy for better visibility, side-mounted control stick to ease control while maneuvering.
4: The Lockheed Martin F-22 Raptor is a fifth-generation, single-seat, twin-engine, all-weather stealth tactical fighter aircraft developed for the United States Air Force.
The aircraft was designed primarily as an air superiority fighter, but also has ground attack, electronic warfare, and signals intelligence capabilities.
5: The Dassault Rafale is a French twin-engine, canard delta wing, multirole fighter aircraft designed and built by Dassault Aviation.

Equipped with a wide range of weapons, the Rafale is intended to perform air supremacy, interdiction, aerial reconnaissance, ground support, in-depth strike, anti-ship strike and nuclear deterrence missions.
6: The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress is a long-range, subsonic, jet-powered strategic bomber.
The B-52 was designed and built by Boeing, which has continued to provide support and upgrades.
7: The Saab JAS 39 Gripen is a light single-engine multirole fighter aircraft manufactured by the Swedish aerospace company Saab.
It was designed to replace the Saab 35 Draken and 37 Viggen in the Swedish Air Force.

topic.name: Major League Baseball Players
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various professional baseball players who have played in Major League Baseball (MLB) and their careers.

Document 1: Ramón Ramírez is a Dominican relief pitcher for the Tigres de Quintana Roo of the Mexican Baseball League.

He pitched for the Hiroshima Toyo Carp in Nippon Professional Baseball (NPB), and the Colorado Rockies, Kansas City Royals, Boston Red Sox, San Francisco Giants, New York Mets and Baltimore Orioles in Major League Baseball (MLB).
Document 2: Gabby Hartnett was an American professional baseball player and manager.
He played almost his entire career in Major League Baseball as a catcher for the Chicago Cubs from 1922 to 1940.
Document 3: John Bowker is an American professional baseball outfielder and first baseman who is a free agent.
He has played for the San Francisco Giants, Pittsburgh Pirates and Philadelphia Phillies of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Document 4: Phil Hughes is an American right-handed professional baseball pitcher for the Minnesota Twins of Major League Baseball (MLB).
He played in MLB for the New York Yankees from 2007 through 2013.
Document 5: Jon Lieber is a former Major League Baseball (MLB) pitcher.
He played for the Pittsburgh Pirates, Chicago Cubs, New York Yankees, and Philadelphia Phillies.
Document 6: Pat Burrell is an American former professional baseball outfielder who played in Major League Baseball.
He played with the Philadelphia Phillies, the Tampa Bay Rays, and the San Francisco Giants.
Document 7: Andy Hansen was a right-handed pitcher in Major League Baseball.
He played for the New York Giants and the Philadelphia Phillies.

topic.name: Urban Rail Systems
topic.description: The documents discuss various urban rail systems and their historical development in cities like Bristol and Oslo.

Document 1: Stapleton Road railway station is on the Severn Beach Line and Cross Country Route, serving the inner-city district of Easton in Bristol, England.
The station was opened in 1863 by the Bristol and South Wales Union Railway.
Document 2: The Oslo Metro is the rapid transit system of Oslo, Norway, operated by Sporveien T-banen.
The first rapid transit line, the Holmenkoll Line, opened in 1898.
Document 3: Lawrence Hill railway station is on the Severn Beach Line and Cross Country Route, serving the inner-city districts of Easton and Lawrence Hill in Bristol, England.
The station was opened in 1863 by the Bristol and South Wales Union Railway.
Document 4: Yatton railway station, on the Bristol to Exeter Line, is in the village of Yatton in North Somerset, England.
It was opened in 1841 by the Bristol and Exeter Railway.
Document 5: Filton Abbey Wood railway station serves the town of Filton in South Gloucestershire, England, inside the Bristol conurbation.
The station was closed in 1903, replaced by a new station, Filton Junction.
Document 6: Oslo Commuter Rail is a commuter rail centered in Oslo, Norway, connecting the capital to six counties in Eastern Norway.
The network spans eight routes and 128 stations, with Oslo Central Station as the central hub.
Document 7: The history of the Oslo Tramway and Oslo Metro in Oslo starts in 1875, when Kristiania Sporveisselskab opened two horsecar lines through the city centre.
The Holmenkollen Line was the first light rail line, which opened in 1898.

topic.name: Medieval Balkan Politics
topic.description: The documents discuss significant figures and events in the political landscape of medieval Bulgaria and Hungary, highlighting rebellions, dynastic struggles, and the influence of external powers.

Document 1: rebellion of the Bulgarian peasantry against the incompetent rule of Emperor Constantine Tikh
the weakness of the state institutions was a result of the accelerating process of feudalisation of the Bulgarian Empire
Document 2: Michael was a descendant of Alexios I Komnenos and a cousin of emperors Isaac II Angelos and Alexios III Angelos
his domain in Epirus became a refuge and centre of resistance of the Greeks against the Latin Crusaders
Document 3: Charles I was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1308 to his death
he came to the Kingdom of Hungary upon the invitation of an influential Croatian lord
Document 4: Andrew I descended from a younger branch of the Árpád dynasty
he strengthened the position of Christianity in the Kingdom of Hungary
Document 5: Stephen V was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1270 and 1272
a civil war broke out between father and son
Document 6: Louis I was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1342 and King of Poland from 1370
he inherited a centralized kingdom and a rich treasury from his father
Document 7: Andrew II was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1205 and 1235
his rule was unpopular, and the boyars expelled him

topic.name: Royal Australian Air Force History
topic.description: The documents discuss the formation, operations, and evolution of various units within the Royal Australian Air Force during and after World War II.

Document 1: The 2nd Parachute Brigade was an airborne forces brigade formed by the British Army during the Second World War.
the brigade saw active service in Italy, the South of France and Greece.
Document 2: North-Western Area Command was one of several geographically based commands raised by the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) during World War II.
Its wartime sphere of operations included the Northern Territory, adjacent portions of Queensland and Western Australia.
Document 3: No. 75 Squadron is a Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) fighter unit based at RAAF Base Tindal in the Northern Territory.
The squadron was formed in 1942 and saw extensive action in the South West Pacific theatre of World War II.
Document 4: No. 6 Squadron is a Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) training and bomber squadron.
It subsequently saw combat as a light bomber and maritime patrol squadron during World War II.
Document 5: No. 86 Wing is a Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) transport and air-to-air refuelling wing.
Formed in the last year of World War II to undertake ground attack missions.
Document 6: No. 81 Wing is the wing responsible for operating the McDonnell Douglas F/A-18 Hornet multi-role fighters of the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF).
Formed in April 1944, No. 81 Wing comprised three flying units, Nos. 76, 77 and 82 Squadrons.
Document 7: No. 78 Wing is a Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) operational training wing.
Formed as a fighter wing in November 1943, No. 78 Wing comprised three flying units.

topic.name: Music Album Releases
topic.description: The documents discuss various music albums by different artists, highlighting their production, themes, and reception.

Document 1: Time on Earth is the fifth studio album by the pop-rock band Crowded House.
the album garnered positive critical reception and reached Platinum status within weeks of release in Australia.
Document 2: Shin-shiro is the third studio album by Japanese band Sakanaction.
the album became the group 's first release to chart in the top 10 albums on Oricon 's weekly album chart.
Document 3: Stone Rollin ' is the fourth studio album by American recording artist Raphael Saadiq.
deemed by some reviewers as Saadiq 's best work.
Document 4: Until the Quiet Comes is the fourth studio album by American electronic producer Flying Lotus.
features free jazz elements, varying musical tones, contracting scale, and shifts in feel.
Document 5: Night Fishing is the second studio album by Japanese band Sakanaction.
critics praising the album 's mix of pop and electronic musical elements.
Document 6: Revelation is the ninth studio album by American Christian rock band Third Day.
many critics regarded it as one of Third Day 's best studio albums.
Document 7: Documentaly is the fifth studio album by Japanese band Sakanaction.
the band were inspired by personal and world events in 2011 to create material for the album.

topic.name: Contemporary Pop and R&B Songs
topic.description: The documents discuss various popular songs from contemporary R&B and pop artists, highlighting their themes, musical styles, and chart performances.

Document 1: 'Look at Me Now' is a song by American singer Chris Brown, featuring American rappers Lil Wayne and Busta Rhymes.
Musically, 'Look at Me Now' is a 'Dirty south-inspired' hip hop song.
Document 2: 'Always Be My Baby' is a song by American singer and songwriter Mariah Carey, taken from her fifth studio album, Daydream.
The song became Carey's 11th chart topper on the Billboard Hot 100.
Document 3: 'Diva' is a song by American singer and songwriter Beyoncé, which delivers messages of independence and female empowerment.
Despite being released to rhythmic and urban radios only in the US, the song peaked at number 19 on the US Billboard Hot 100 chart.
Document 4: 'Cheers (Drink to That)' is a song recorded by Barbadian recording artist Rihanna, from her fifth studio album, Loud.
The song has received positive reviews from music critics, who praised the interpolation of Lavigne's 'I'm with You'.
Document 5: 'Video Phone' is a song by American singer Beyoncé from her third studio album, I Am ... Sasha Fierce.
The original version charted in the lower regions of the charts of Australia and its Urban chart.
Document 6: 'Who's That Chick?' is a song by French DJ David Guetta featuring guest vocals by Barbadian recording artist Rihanna.
Guetta was asked to produce songs for Rihanna's fifth studio album, Loud.
Document 7: 'Take a Bow' is a song recorded by Barbadian singer Rihanna for Good Girl Gone Bad: Reloaded.
The song peaked at number one on the Billboard Hot 100 chart and has been certified quadruple platinum.

topic.name: Medieval Christian Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss various medieval Christian figures, primarily bishops and archbishops, and their contributions to the church and society during their time.

Document 1: Romanus was the second bishop of Rochester and presumably was a member of the Gregorian mission sent to Kent to Christianize the Anglo-Saxons.
He drowned in the Mediterranean Sea off Italy while on a mission to Rome for Justus.
Document 3: Æthelnoth was a medieval Archbishop of Canterbury.
He travelled to Rome and brought back saint's relics.
Document 4: Robert of Cricklade was a medieval English writer and prior of St Frideswide's Priory in Oxford.
He wrote a number of theological works as well as a lost biography of Thomas Becket.
Document 6: Bregowine was a medieval Archbishop of Canterbury.
He was consecrated as archbishop on 27 September 761.
Document 7: Caffo was a sixth-century Christian in Anglesey, north Wales, who is venerated as a saint and martyr.
He is said to have been one of the sons of St Caw, a king in northern Britain.

topic.name: Major Battles of American Wars
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant battles and military campaigns during the American Revolutionary War and the American Civil War.

Document 1: The Battle of Antietam, fought on September 17, 1862, was the first major battle in the American Civil War to take place on Union soil.
It is the bloodiest single-day battle in American history, with a combined tally of 22,717 dead, wounded, or missing.
Document 2: The Saratoga Campaign in 1777 was an attempt by the British high command for North America to gain military control of the strategically important Hudson River valley during the American Revolutionary War.
It ended in the surrender of the British army, which historian Edmund Morgan argues, was a great turning point of the war.
Document 3: The Yorktown campaign was a series of military maneuvers and battles during the American Revolutionary War that culminated in the decisive Siege of Yorktown in October 1781.
The result of the campaign was the surrender of the British Army force of General Charles, Earl Cornwallis.
Document 4: The Battle of Long Island was the first major battle of the American Revolutionary War to take place after the United States declared its independence.
It was a victory for the British Army and the beginning of a successful campaign that gave them control of the strategically important city of New York.
Document 5: The Battles of Lexington and Concord were the first military engagements of the American Revolutionary War.
The battles marked the outbreak of open armed conflict between the Kingdom of Great Britain and thirteen of its colonies on the mainland of British America.

topic.name: Motor Racing Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various significant motor racing events, including NASCAR and Formula One races, highlighting race details, winners, and key moments.

Document 1: The 2008 UAW-Dodge 400 was the third stock car race of the 2008 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series.
The race was won by Carl Edwards of the Roush Fenway Racing team.
Document 2: The 2010 Singapore Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 26 September 2010.
The race was won by Ferrari driver Fernando Alonso who started in pole position.
Document 3: The 2011 Daytona 500 was held on February 20, 2011 at Daytona International Speedway.
Trevor Bayne, driving for Wood Brothers Racing, won the race becoming the youngest Daytona 500 winner.
Document 4: The 2000 Belgian Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 27 August 2000.
It was won by McLaren driver Mika Häkkinen, who started from the pole position.
Document 5: The 2000 French Grand Prix was held on 2 July 2000 at the Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours.
It was won by McLaren driver David Coulthard after starting from second position.
Document 6: The 2006 Bank of America 500 was the 31st stock car race of the 2006 NASCAR Nextel Cup Series.
It was held on October 14, 2006, before a crowd of 175,000 in Concord, North Carolina.
Document 7: The 2001 German Grand Prix was held on 29 July 2001 at the Hockenheimring.
It was won by Williams driver Ralf Schumacher after starting from the second position.

topic.name: Constitutional Rights and Protections
topic.description: The documents discuss various constitutional rights and protections, focusing on citizenship, equality, freedom of speech, and legal procedures in the context of the United States and Singapore.

Document 1: addresses citizenship rights and equal protection of the laws
is one of the most litigated parts of the Constitution
Document 2: contains several provisions regarding the law of criminal procedure
all of the criminal procedure provisions of the Bill of Rights have been incorporated to apply to the state governments
Document 3: guarantees freedom of religion in Singapore
Every person has the right to profess and practise his religion
Document 4: guarantees to all persons equality before the law
discrimination on those classifications is prohibited
Document 5: asserting that the imposition , assessment and collection of the federal income tax violates the United States Constitution
Some protesters argue that imposition of income taxes violates the First Amendment
Document 6: guarantees to Singapore citizens the rights to freedom of speech and expression
enjoyment of these rights may be restricted by laws imposed by the Parliament of Singapore
Document 7: grants the executive power to enforce preventive detention
the Court of Appeal sought to impose legal limits on the power of preventive detention

topic.name: Animated and Sci-Fi Films
topic.description: This cluster discusses various animated and science fiction films, highlighting their production, storylines, and critical reception.

1: Frozen is a 2013 American 3D computer-animated musical fantasy comedy film produced by Walt Disney Animation Studios.
The film tells the story of a fearless princess who sets off on an epic journey alongside a rugged iceman.
2: Star Trek: Insurrection is a 1998 American science fiction film released by Paramount Pictures.
The crew of the USS Enterprise-E rebel against Starfleet after they discover a conspiracy.
3: Transformers: Dark of the Moon is a 2011 American science fiction action film directed by Michael Bay.
The film's story is set three years after the events of the 2009 film.
4: Toy Story is a 1995 American computer-animated adventure buddy comedy film produced by Pixar Animation Studios.
Toy Story follows a group of anthropomorphic toys who pretend to be lifeless whenever humans are present.
5: Guardians of the Galaxy is a 2014 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics superhero team.
Peter Quill forms an uneasy alliance with a group of extraterrestrial misfits.
6: Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace is a 1999 American epic space opera film written and directed by George Lucas.
The film follows Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn and his apprentice Obi-Wan Kenobi as they protect Queen Amidala.
7: Star Trek is an American science fiction entertainment franchise created by Gene Roddenberry.
The first series, now referred to as The Original Series, debuted in 1966 and ran for three seasons on NBC.

topic.name: Medieval Parish Churches in Wales and England
topic.description: The documents discuss various medieval parish churches located in Wales and England, highlighting their historical significance, architectural features, and current status.

Document 1: St Eugrad's Church is an isolated church near the village of Marian-glas, in Anglesey, north Wales.
It contains a 12th-century font, a 13th-century carved stone depicting the crucifixion.
Document 2: St Mary's Church is an Anglican parish church in the village of Newbold Astbury, Cheshire, England.
It is possible that a church was present on the site in the Saxon era, although the earliest fabric in the church is Norman.
Document 3: St Edern's Church, Bodedern is a medieval parish church in the village of Bodedern, in Anglesey, north Wales.
The oldest parts of the present building date from the 14th century.
Document 4: St Margaret's Church is an Anglican church in the Ifield neighbourhood of Crawley, a town and borough in West Sussex, England.
The present building incorporates the chancel from a 13th-century church.
Document 5: St Mary's Church, Llanfair-yng-Nghornwy is a medieval parish church in the north-west of Anglesey, north Wales.
The oldest parts date from the 11th or 12th century.
Document 6: St Beuno's Church is the medieval parish church of Trefdraeth, a hamlet in Anglesey, north Wales.
The oldest parts of the present building date from the 13th century.
Document 7: Chester Cathedral is a Church of England cathedral and the mother church of the Diocese of Chester.
The cathedral is a Grade I listed building, and part of a heritage site that also includes the former monastic buildings.

topic.name: U.S. Highways and State Routes
topic.description: This topic encompasses the descriptions, routes, and historical context of various U.S. highways and state routes across different states.

Document 1: U.S. Route 64 is a U.S. highway running from the Four Corners area to the Outer Banks of North Carolina.
US-64 serves fifteen Oklahoma counties and the cities of Guymon, Woodward, Enid, and Muskogee.
Document 2: Maryland Route 5 is a 74.34-mile long state highway that runs north – south in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The route merges onto U.S. Route 301 (US 301, Crain Highway) and enters Prince George's County.
Document 3: Interstate 70 is a part of the Interstate Highway System that runs from Cove Fort, Utah to Baltimore, Maryland.
I-70 runs concurrently with its predecessor highway, U.S. Route 40 (US 40).
Document 4: Delaware Route 1 is a 103.02-mile-long state highway in the U.S. state of Delaware.
The route runs from the Maryland border in Fenwick Island, Sussex County, where it continues into that state as Maryland Route 528.
Document 5: There are currently eight business routes of Interstate 94 in the US state of Michigan.
These business routes connect I-94 to the downtown business districts of neighboring cities.
Document 6: Maryland Route 2 is the longest state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The route runs concurrent with MD 4 through much of Calvert County along a four-lane divided highway.
Document 7: U.S. Route 13 is a U.S. highway running from Fayetteville, North Carolina north to Morrisville, Pennsylvania.
US 13 connects many important cities and towns in Delaware, including Seaford, Dover, and Wilmington.

topic.name: Mathematics and Physics Concepts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses foundational concepts in mathematics and physics, including calculus, linear algebra, and their applications in understanding physical phenomena.

Document 1: The derivative of a function of a real variable measures the sensitivity to change of a quantity
Derivatives are a fundamental tool of calculus.
Document 2: In mathematics, a matrix is a rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions
A major application of matrices is to represent linear transformations.
Document 3: The Dirac delta function is a generalized function on the real number line
The delta function only makes sense as a mathematical object when it appears inside an integral.
Document 4: In physics, a force is any interaction that, when unopposed, will change the motion of an object
A force has both magnitude and direction, making it a vector quantity.
Document 5: A vector space is a collection of objects called vectors
Euclidean vectors are an example of a vector space.
Document 6: The mathematical concept of a Hilbert space generalizes the notion of Euclidean space
Hilbert spaces arise naturally and frequently in mathematics and physics.
Document 7: In electronics, negative resistance is a property of some electrical circuits
Negative resistance produces power.

topic.name: Culinary and Cultural Influences
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of culinary arts, cultural contributions, and historical figures associated with food and entertainment.

1: I Love Bacon ! is a cookbook with over fifty recipes devoted to bacon and bacon dishes
the book was written by Jayne Rockmill and photography was provided by Ben Fink.
2: Every Sunday is a 1936 American short musical film
the film tells the story of two young girls and their efforts to save a public concert series.

topic.name: 19th Century Ironclad Warships
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of ironclad warships built for the French and British navies during the 19th century, focusing on their designs, specifications, and historical roles.

Document 1: The Colbert class were a pair of armored frigates built for the French Navy during the 1870s.
They took part in the French conquest of Tunisia, notably shelling and landing troops in Sfax in 1881.
Document 2: The Hector-class ironclads were a pair of armoured frigates built for the Royal Navy in the 1860s.
They were mobilized during the Russo-Turkish War of 1877 – 78, but saw no action.
Document 3: HMS Defence was the lead ship of the Defence-class armoured frigates ordered by the Royal Navy in 1859.
The Defence-class ironclads were designed as smaller and cheaper versions of the Warrior-class armoured frigates.
Document 4: The Océan class ironclads were a group of three wooden-hulled armored frigates built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s.
Marengo participated in the French conquest of Tunisia in 1881.
Document 5: Suffren was a wooden-hulled, armored frigate of the Océan class, built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s.
The ships were central battery ironclads, with the armament concentrated amidships.
Document 6: The French ironclad Trident was the second and last ship of the Colbert-class ironclads that were built for the French Navy in the 1870s.
Trident was reclassified as a training ship in 1894 and condemned in 1900.
Document 7: Océan was a wooden-hulled, armored frigate built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s and the lead ship of her class.
She was commissioned in 1870 to participate in the Franco-Prussian War.

topic.name: Mycena Mushroom Species
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of mushrooms belonging to the Mycena genus, primarily found in Japan, detailing their physical characteristics, habitats, and taxonomic classifications.

Document 1: Mycena mustea is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
First described as a new species in 2007.
Document 2: Mycena maculata, commonly known as the reddish-spotted Mycena, is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
The species was first described scientifically by the German mycologist Petter Karsten in 1890.
Document 3: Gyroporus cyanescens, commonly known as the bluing bolete or the cornflower bolete, is a species of bolete fungus.
The species is found in Asia, Australia, Europe, and eastern North America.
Document 4: Mycena fuscoaurantiaca is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
First reported as a new species in 2007.
Document 5: Mycena intersecta is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
First reported as a new species in 2007.
Document 6: Mycena clariviolacea is a mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
First reported as a new species in 2007.
Document 7: Mycena lanuginosa is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
First collected in 2000 and reported as a new species in 2007.

topic.name: Bolliger & Mabillard Roller Coasters
topic.description: This topic covers the design, history, and features of roller coasters manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard, a prominent roller coaster design consultancy.

Document 1: Goliath is a steel coaster roller coaster at La Ronde located in Montreal, Quebec, Canada.
Designed by Bolliger & Mabillard, it reaches a maximum height of 174.8 feet.
Document 2: Afterburn is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Carowinds amusement park.
Designed by Bolliger & Mabillard, Afterburn stands 113 feet tall and reaches speeds of 62 miles per hour.
Document 3: Nemesis is an inverted roller coaster located at the Alton Towers amusement park in England.
Manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard and opened in March 1994.
Document 4: Bolliger & Mabillard, abbreviated B & M, is a roller coaster design consultancy based in Monthey, Switzerland.
Since 1988, B & M has built 100 roller coasters around the world.
Document 5: Batman: The Ride is a steel inverted roller coaster found in Six Flags theme parks.
Built by consulting engineers Bolliger & Mabillard, it rises to a height of between 100 and 105 feet.
Document 6: Nitro is a steel Floorless Coaster at the Adlabs Imagica amusement park located in Khopoli, Mumbai, India.
Manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard, the roller coaster reaches a maximum height of 132 feet.
Document 7: Tatsu is a steel flying roller coaster designed by Bolliger & Mabillard at the Six Flags Magic Mountain amusement park.
Tatsu reaches a height of 170 feet and speeds up to 62 miles per hour.

topic.name: Music Rhythm Video Games
topic.description: This topic encompasses music rhythm video games that allow players to simulate playing musical instruments and perform popular songs, focusing on titles like Guitar Hero and Rock Band.

1: Guitar Hero 5 is a music rhythm game and the fifth main entry in the Guitar Hero series.
The game is geared towards playing in a four-person band experience, including lead and bass guitar, drums, and vocals.
3: Guitar Hero World Tour is a music rhythm game developed by Neversoft, published by Activision.
The game allows users to create new songs through the 'Music Studio' mode.
4: Rock Band allows up to four players to simulate the performance of popular rock music songs by playing with controllers modeled after musical instruments.
Players can play the lead guitar, bass guitar, and drums parts to songs with 'instrument controllers'.
7: The Guitar Hero series is a series of music rhythm games first published in 2005 by RedOctane and Harmonix.
Players use a guitar-shaped game controller to simulate playing lead, bass guitar, and rhythm guitar across numerous rock music songs.

topic.name: Rivers and Waterways in Europe
topic.description: This topic covers various rivers and waterways in Europe, detailing their geographical significance, navigability, historical uses, and ecological importance.

Document 1: The Adriatic Sea is a body of water separating the Italian Peninsula from the Balkan peninsula.
The prevailing currents flow counterclockwise from the Strait of Otranto.
Document 2: The River Weaver is a river, navigable in its lower reaches, running in a curving route anti-clockwise across west Cheshire.
Improvements to the river to make it navigable were authorised in 1720.
Document 3: The River Tone is a river in the English county of Somerset.
An act of Parliament granted in 1699 authorised work that made the river navigable as far as Taunton.
Document 4: The Sava is a river in Central Europe, a right side tributary of the Danube.
The Sava is one of the longest rivers in Europe and among a handful of European rivers of that length that do not drain directly into a sea.
Document 5: The River Rother flows for 35 miles through the English counties of East Sussex and Kent.
The river has been used for navigation since Roman times.
Document 6: The River Rother flows from Empshott in Hampshire, England, to Stopham in West Sussex.
The lower river, from Midhurst to its junction with the River Arun, has been used for navigation.
Document 7: The River Welland is a lowland river in the east of England, some 65 miles long.
Significant improvements were made to the river in the 1660s, when a new cut with 10 locks was constructed.

topic.name: Atlantic Tropical Cyclones
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclones, specifically hurricanes and tropical storms, that have impacted the Atlantic region, detailing their formation, paths, intensities, and effects on land and populations.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Claudette was the third named storm of the 2009 Atlantic hurricane season
the storm weakened to a tropical depression and the Hydrometeorological Prediction Center took over primary responsibility of the storm.
Document 2: Hurricane Anita was a powerful Atlantic hurricane during an otherwise quiet 1977 Atlantic hurricane season
the hurricane caused strong winds and moderate rainfall.
Document 3: Tropical Storm Danielle was a weak tropical storm that made landfall on Virginia
the storm dropped light rainfall throughout its path.
Document 4: the 1920 Louisiana hurricane was a strong tropical cyclone that caused significant damage in parts of Louisiana
the hurricane generated strong winds along a wide swath of the coast.
Document 5: the 1926 Louisiana hurricane caused widespread devastation to the United States Gulf Coast
the hurricane's strong storm surge at landfall caused extensive damage to coastal regions.
Document 6: Tropical Storm Dolly was a short-lived and disorganized tropical cyclone that caused moderate damage across Tamaulipas, Mexico
the storm produced widespread moderate to heavy rain in Mexico.
Document 7: Hurricane Juliette was the strongest hurricane and final tropical cyclone of the inactive 1995 Pacific hurricane season
the hurricane reached peak winds of 150 mph.
